rules.name: Missing ownership check on resource endpoint
rules.prompt: Check if this route handler verifies that the authenticated user owns the requested resource (e.g., order.userId === req.user.id) before returning or modifying it.
rules.message: No ownership verification found. User A can access User B's data by guessing IDs.

rules.name: Mass assignment of privileged fields
rules.prompt: Trace req.body into the ORM update/create call. Are sensitive fields like role, isAdmin, verified, or plan exposed to user input?
rules.message: Users can set their own role or admin status by including it in the request body.

rules.name: Auth check after sensitive action
rules.prompt: Is the authorization check executed before or after the sensitive operation? Could an early return or exception bypass the check?
rules.message: Authorization check comes after the data is already read/written — it can be bypassed.

rules.name: Password reset token not invalidated after use
rules.prompt: Trace the password reset flow. After the token is consumed and the password changed, is the token deleted or marked used in the database?
rules.message: Password reset link is reusable — allows repeated account takeover attempts.

rules.name: Auth token not revoked on logout
rules.prompt: Does the logout handler invalidate the token server-side (blacklist, DB revocation, session deletion)?
rules.message: Logout doesn't invalidate the token server-side — stolen tokens work indefinitely.

rules.name: Password change does not invalidate sessions
rules.prompt: After a password change, are existing sessions or JWTs for the user invalidated?
rules.message: Old sessions remain valid after a password change — attackers retain access even after the victim resets.

rules.name: MFA bypass via direct API call
rules.prompt: Map all API endpoints that perform privileged actions. Is MFA enforcement applied at the middleware level on all of them, or only on the frontend flow?
rules.message: MFA can be bypassed by calling the API directly, skipping the frontend MFA step.

rules.name: Negative quantity / price not validated
rules.prompt: Check whether the purchase or cart endpoint enforces that quantity and price are positive numbers before processing.
rules.message: Negative quantities can result in refunds or credits being issued to the attacker.

rules.name: Price loaded from client instead of database
rules.prompt: In the checkout/order creation flow, is the price fetched from the database or trusted from req.body?
rules.message: User can set their own price by including it in the request body. Always fetch price from DB.

rules.name: Webhook event not idempotent
rules.prompt: Does the webhook handler check an idempotency key or event ID to prevent processing the same event twice?
rules.message: Duplicate webhook delivery (common in Stripe/PayPal) can cause double-credits or double-charges.

rules.name: Subscription feature gate enforced only on frontend
rules.prompt: Find all premium feature restrictions. Are they enforced server-side in the API, or only hidden from the UI?
rules.message: Frontend-only feature gates can be bypassed by calling the API directly.

rules.name: Inventory not decremented atomically on purchase
rules.prompt: Is the inventory decrement and the order creation done atomically (in a DB transaction)? Can two simultaneous purchases oversell the last item?
rules.message: Race condition allows overselling — two users can buy the last item simultaneously.

rules.name: API response exposes sensitive fields
rules.prompt: Review all API response serializers. Do they return internal fields like password_hash, stripe_customer_id, internal_notes, or raw IDs that should not be client-visible?
rules.message: API returns more data than the client needs, exposing internal infrastructure or private user data.

rules.name: Unbounded pagination limit
rules.prompt: Do paginated endpoints enforce a maximum value for the limit/pageSize query parameter? Can a user request 100,000 records in one call?
rules.message: No upper bound on pagination allows a single request to dump the entire database.

rules.name: User enumeration via distinct error messages or timing
rules.prompt: Compare the error messages and response timing for 'user not found' vs 'wrong password'. Do they differ in a way that reveals whether an account exists?
rules.message: Distinct error messages (or response timing) reveal whether an email is registered.

rules.name: Prompt injection via unsanitized user input
rules.prompt: Trace how user input flows into the LLM API call (system prompt, messages array). Is raw user content concatenated into the system prompt without isolation?
rules.message: User can inject instructions into your AI system prompt, overriding your app's behavior.

rules.name: LLM used as a security gate
rules.prompt: Is an LLM being asked to make a trust or safety decision (e.g., 'is this request malicious?')? LLMs can be manipulated via prompt injection to return the wrong answer.
rules.message: LLMs should not be used as security gates — they can be manipulated into approving malicious requests.

rules.name: LLM output rendered to DOM without sanitization
rules.prompt: Trace LLM API response output through to the DOM render. Is it sanitized before being rendered as HTML?
rules.message: LLM output rendered as raw HTML can contain attacker-injected scripts via prompt injection.

rules.name: No rate limiting on AI-powered endpoints
rules.prompt: Identify all routes that make LLM API calls. Do they have rate limiting middleware to prevent a single user from exhausting the API budget?
rules.message: Without rate limits, a single user can make thousands of LLM calls and drain your API credits.

rules.name: User data sent to third-party LLM without disclosure
rules.prompt: Does the app send user-provided data (names, emails, messages) to an external LLM API? Is there a privacy policy or user consent mechanism?
rules.message: User data sent to OpenAI/Anthropic without consent may violate GDPR and privacy expectations.

rules.name: Tenant data leakage across organizations
rules.prompt: For every database query in the codebase, verify that it is scoped to the current user's orgId or tenantId. Is it possible for User A in Org 1 to query data belonging to Org 2?
rules.message: Missing tenant scoping allows one organization to access another's data.

rules.name: Cross-tenant search without tenant filter
rules.prompt: Does the search endpoint filter results by the authenticated user's tenantId? Or does it search across all tenant data?
rules.message: Search queries without tenant filtering expose all organizations' data to any authenticated user.

rules.name: SSRF via user-supplied URL
rules.prompt: Identify any route where the server makes an HTTP request to a URL that was provided by the user. Is the URL validated against an allowlist of safe domains?
rules.message: Server-Side Request Forgery — attacker can make your server fetch internal AWS metadata or internal services.

rules.name: OAuth redirect URI not validated
rules.prompt: In the OAuth callback or redirect flow, is the redirect_uri parameter validated against a strict allowlist before redirecting the user?
rules.message: An open redirect in OAuth allows attackers to steal authorization codes.
